searchDocs.title: Configure​
searchDocs.content: </> Facet.Configure(overrides: { ObjectPooling: boolean?, Multithreading: boolean?, GreedyMeshing: boolean? }) → () Merges feature-flag overrides into Facet's shared Config - ObjectPooling (default false), Multithreading (default true), GreedyMeshing (default true). Call it once, anywhere, before or while using Facet; every controller reads the same table, so flipping a flag takes effect on the next Shatter/Cleanup call, not just ones from controllers defined afterward.

searchDocs.title: Define​
searchDocs.content: </> Facet.Define(config: DefineConfig) → VoxelController Creates a VoxelController. Type picks Relative (dynamic sizing via RecommendedVoxelSize/MinimumVoxelSize, multithreaded, swappable Drivers) or Absolute (fixed VoxelSize). Pass Host to scope every Shatter call on the controller to one instance without naming a Target each time.

searchDocs.title: configure​
searchDocs.content: </> Config.configure(overrides: { ObjectPooling: boolean?, Multithreading: boolean?, GreedyMeshing: boolean? }) → () Merges overrides into the shared config table - call it once, anywhere, before or while using Facet; every controller reads the same table. Unknown keys error immediately instead of being silently ignored.

searchDocs.title: ShatterSizeConfig​
searchDocs.content: </> interface ShatterSizeConfig { Effects: {[string]: any}?-- same shape as ShatterConfig.Effects. Size: number?-- overrides the controller's own sizing rule for just this re-shatter. }

searchDocs.title: computeAsync​
searchDocs.content: </> Workers.computeAsync(jobs: {Job}) → {{Voxelize.MergedVoxel}}

interface Job { Grid: Grid-- a part's already-built grid (Voxelize.buildGrid) - plain data, no Instance reference. Hitbox: PointHitbox?-- the classification hitbox, in the same Instance-free shape Voxelize.classifyPoint expects. nil means "no hitbox" - every cell classifies Partial. GreedyMeshing: boolean?-- Config.GreedyMeshing, read on the calling thread and carried across the Actor boundary as plain data (defaults true). }  Runs Voxelize.computeCells() for every job in jobs, spread across the Actor pool, and returns the results in the same order jobs was given. Falls back to running every job serially on the calling thread (still correct, just not parallel) if the Actor pool isn't available.

searchDocs.title: HitboxConfig​
searchDocs.content: </> interface HitboxConfig { Size: Vector3? -- box/region extents, in studs, centered on CFrame. Ignored by Raycast. CFrame: CFrame? -- where the hitbox sits in the world. Defaults to CFrame.new() (the origin) if omitted. ViewVector: Vector3? -- cast direction for QueryType.Raycast only; ignored by every other QueryType. Color: Color3? -- cosmetic only - tints a visualized hitbox if you render one yourself. Facet never draws it for you. QueryType: Enums.QueryType?-- Box (default) | Sphere | Region | Raycast. Picks which exact-shape test runs after the octree broad phase. HitboxType: Enums.HitboxType?-- Partial (default, voxelizes whatever's detected) | Whole (returns fully-contained parts untouched, no slicing). Shape: Enum.PartType?-- reserved for a future visualized-hitbox helper; not read by Shatter today. Copy: Instance? -- reserved for a future "clone the hitbox as a real part" helper; not read by Shatter today. Scope: Instance? -- only read when Shatter is called with no Target and no Host (indiscriminate destruction) - restricts the workspace-wide query to this instance's descendants. Defaults to the whole workspace. Ignored otherwise (a Target/Host already scopes the search). }

searchDocs.title: CleanupConfig​
searchDocs.content: </> interface CleanupConfig { Method: (Enums.Cleanup | (Voxel) → ())?-- Rebuild | Fade | Destroy, or your own function(voxel) ... end. Only ever runs against Result.Voxels.Partial. Delay: number?-- seconds the Cleanup tween takes (Fade/Rebuild only - Destroy is instant). Defaults to 0.5 for Fade, 0.6 for Rebuild. }

searchDocs.title: DefineConfig​
searchDocs.content: </> interface DefineConfig { Type: Enums.ControllerType-- Relative (dynamic sizing, requires RecommendedVoxelSize) or Absolute (fixed VoxelSize). Required. RuntimeType: Enums.RuntimeType?-- Instant (default, runs every Shatter immediately) or Timeline (queues calls until :Start()). Host: Instance? -- scopes every Shatter call on this controller to one instance without naming a Target each time. MinimumVoxelSize: number?-- Relative only. Floor Shatter will shrink voxels to under load. Defaults to RecommendedVoxelSize (no shrink) if omitted. RecommendedVoxelSize: number?-- Relative only. Target voxel size the controller sizes toward. Required for Relative. Drivers: {VoxelizationMethod: string?}?-- reserved for swapping the slicing backend; not read by Shatter today. VoxelSize: number?-- Absolute only. The fixed cube size every voxel is sliced to. Required for Absolute. }

searchDocs.title: Shatter​
searchDocs.content: </> VoxelController:Shatter( a: Instance | ShatterConfig,-- a Target when the controller has no Host, otherwise the ShatterConfig directly. b: ShatterConfig?-- the config, when a was a Target. ) → PendingResult-- chain off it with :Next(Enums.Event.X):Then(callback).

interface ShatterConfig { Hitbox: HitboxConfig?-- scopes detection to a region of the target. Omit entirely to voxelize the whole target. Effects: {[string]: any}?-- keyed by Enums.Effects - see Enums.Effects for the full list and each one's config shape. Cleanup: CleanupConfig?-- { Method = Enums.Cleanup.X, Delay = number? }, or your own { Method = function(voxel) ... end }. }  Shatter(Config) when the controller has a [Host], Target is implied. Shatter(Target, Config) otherwise. Hitbox is optional in either form - omit it to shatter the whole Target/Host directly with no detection step. A third form: Shatter(Config) with no Host and a Hitbox runs indiscriminate destruction - there's no single Target, so the hitbox queries hitbox.Scope (default the whole workspace) directly and shatters whatever it finds, regardless of which part it came from. Result.Instance is nil for this form, since no one Target/Host exists.

searchDocs.title: Start​
searchDocs.content: </> VoxelController:Start() → () Flushes the Timeline queue in call order. No-op for Instant controllers - they never queue, every :Shatter() call already ran by the time it returns.

searchDocs.title: Destroy​
searchDocs.content: </> VoxelController:Destroy() → () Clears any queued Timeline jobs that haven't run yet. Doesn't touch parts already produced by past :Shatter() calls - those live on independently under workspace._Bin.Voxels.

searchDocs.title: ControllerType​
searchDocs.content: </> Enums.ControllerType: { Relative: "Relative", Absolute: "Absolute" } Passed as DefineConfig.Type. Relative sizes dynamically toward RecommendedVoxelSize and splits multi-part Shatter calls across the Workers Actor pool; Absolute uses a fixed VoxelSize and never threads.

searchDocs.title: RuntimeType​
searchDocs.content: </> Enums.RuntimeType: { Instant: "Instant", Timeline: "Timeline" } Passed as DefineConfig.RuntimeType. Instant (default) runs every Shatter call the moment it's called; Timeline queues calls until Controller:Start() flushes them in order.

searchDocs.title: HitboxType​
searchDocs.content: </> Enums.HitboxType: { Whole: "Whole", Partial: "Partial" } Passed as HitboxConfig.HitboxType. Partial (default) slices whatever the hitbox finds; Whole returns parts the hitbox fully contains untouched, with no slicing.

searchDocs.title: QueryType​
searchDocs.content: </> Enums.QueryType: { Raycast: "Raycast", Box: "Box", Sphere: "Sphere", Region: "Region" } Passed as HitboxConfig.QueryType. Mirrors Roblox's own spatial query kinds. Box/Region run through the Octree + an exact box/AABB test, Sphere through the Octree + a distance test, and Raycast casts directly through the engine (single closest hit - see the README's Raycast caveat).

searchDocs.title: Effects​
searchDocs.content: </> Enums.Effects: { Explode: "Explode", Anchored: "Anchored", Chunk: "Chunk", Slice: "Slice", Blackhole: "Blackhole", Whitehole: "Whitehole", Scatter: "Scatter", Shrink: "Shrink" } Keys into ShatterConfig.Effects. See docs/effects for the full config shape each one reads: Explode = {Radius: number} - pushes the voxel outward from the Shatter origin. Anchored = boolean - sets the produced part's Anchored directly. Chunk = boolean | {BreakSpeed: number?, BreakRadius: number?} - welds every voxel a Shatter call produced (Partial and Extra) into one unanchored rigid assembly, so the remainder of a building falls as a single piece once its support is cut away, instead of every voxel tumbling apart individually. A weld breaks - detaching that piece and everything within BreakRadius studs of it (default 4) - the moment its relative impact speed against whatever it hit reaches BreakSpeed studs/sec (default 40), so the assembly fractures into smaller chunks on a hard enough landing instead of staying one rigid lump forever. Slice = {Normal: Vector3?, Speed: number?} - splits voxels either side of a plane (through the Shatter origin, facing Normal) apart along that normal at Speed studs/sec. Blackhole = {Position: Vector3?, Duration: number?} - tweens the voxel into Position while shrinking it to nothing, then destroys/releases it. Whitehole = {Position: Vector3?, Speed: number?} - launches the voxel directly away from Position at Speed studs/sec (like Explode, but a fixed point and speed instead of a radius). Scatter = {Speed: number?} - launches the voxel in a random direction at Speed studs/sec. Shrink = {Duration: number?} - tweens the voxel's Size down to nothing over Duration seconds, then destroys/releases it.

searchDocs.title: Cleanup​
searchDocs.content: </> Enums.Cleanup: { Rebuild: "Rebuild", Fade: "Fade", Destroy: "Destroy" } Passed as ShatterConfig.Cleanup.Method, or to Voxel:Cleanup directly. Destroy is instant; Fade tweens Transparency to 1 then destroys; Rebuild tweens the voxel's CFrame back to OriginalCFrame and never destroys it.

searchDocs.title: Event​
searchDocs.content: </> Enums.Event: { NewVoxel: "NewVoxel", Destroyed: "Destroyed" } Passed to PendingResult:Next. NewVoxel fires once, right after voxelization/Effects; Destroyed fires once every Partial voxel from that call has actually been destroyed by Cleanup (never fires if Cleanup was Rebuild or unset).

searchDocs.title: Strategies​
searchDocs.content: Enums.Cleanup.Destroy - instant. Ignores Delay.Enums.Cleanup.Fade - tweens Transparency to 1 over Delay seconds (default 0.5), then destroys (or, with ObjectPooling on, releases) the part.Enums.Cleanup.Rebuild - anchors the voxel and tweens its CFrame back toOriginalCFrame over Delay seconds (default 0.6), reassembling the target in place instead of destroying anything.a function - Cleanup = { Method = function(voxel) ... end } runs your own logic against each Partial voxel instead. voxel is a full Voxel, so you still getvoxel.Instance, voxel.OriginalCFrame/OriginalPosition/OriginalOrientation, voxel.Kind, and voxel.Distance. Delay is ignored - read whatever you need straight off the function's closure instead.

searchDocs.title: Rebuild/Fade doing nothing visible? Check Anchored​
searchDocs.content: Rebuild and Fade only animate whatever a voxel's current CFrame/Transparency already is - they don't make debris move on their own. A produced voxel inherits the original part'sAnchored state by default (see Effects), so if the part you shattered wasAnchored = true (a typical static wall/building), every voxel comes out anchored too: nothing has a velocity to move with, the debris sits exactly where it was sliced, and Rebuild's tween (which targets that same unmoved position) looks like a no-op. This is the single most common "Cleanup looks broken" report - it's actually an Effects gap, not a Cleanup one.  Fix it by setting Effects.Anchored = false (lets the slice fall/fly under whatever velocity you gave it) or, for an actual structural collapse, Effects.Chunk = true - seeEffects for the full breakdown.

searchDocs.title: Per-voxel cleanup​
searchDocs.content: Every Voxel - in any bucket, not just Partial - exposes the same logic directly. The direct method keeps the simpler two-argument shape (no Cleanup table needed, since there's no second voxel bucket to apply it to):  for _, voxel in result.Voxels.Extra do voxel:Cleanup(Enums.Cleanup.Fade, 2) end

searchDocs.title: Knowing when it's done​
searchDocs.content: Controller:Shatter(...) fires Enums.Event.Destroyed once every Partial voxel from that call has actually been destroyed (not just had Cleanup started on it):  Wall:Shatter(config):Next(Enums.Event.Destroyed):Then(function(result) print("debris is fully gone") end)   This only fires for Destroy/Fade (anything that calls Instance:Destroy(), including theBlackhole/Shrink Effects, which destroy on their own timer independent of Cleanup);Rebuild never destroys the part, so a call with only Rebuild set never fires Destroyed.

searchDocs.title: Result​
searchDocs.content: </> interface Result { Voxels: { Whole: {Voxel}, Partial: {Voxel}, Extra: {Voxel} }-- every produced Voxel, split three ways. Whole: isolated but never sliced (HitboxType.Whole only). Partial: the actual debris - sliced, and the only bucket Cleanup ever runs against. Extra: produced but outside the hitbox - greedy-meshed into a handful of boxes and left alone unless you Cleanup it yourself. Instance: Instance? -- the Target/Host this Shatter call ran against. nil for indiscriminate destruction (no Target, no Host - just a Hitbox). CollisionPoint: Vector3? -- reserved for a future raycast-driven Shatter helper; not populated today. SurfaceNormal: Vector3? -- reserved for a future raycast-driven Shatter helper; not populated today. }

searchDocs.title: Notes on ObjectPooling​
searchDocs.content: Pooling only ever recycles plain Part instances Facet itself created - it never pools the original target/host part you're shattering. A pooled part is reset (Anchored/CanCollide/CanQuery/CanTouch disabled, parented under workspace._Bin.Pool) when released, and restored when reacquired. If you never enable it, Facet behaves exactly as before -Instance.new/Destroy per voxel.

searchDocs.title: Where to go next​
searchDocs.content: Getting started - install Facet and run your first ShatterHitboxes - the four query types, indiscriminate destruction, and how detection worksEffects - Explode, Chunk, Slice, Blackhole, Whitehole, Scatter, Shrink, and why debris sometimes doesn't moveCleanup - Rebuild, Fade, Destroy, and writing your ownConfiguration - turn object pooling, multithreading, and greedy meshing on/off  The full API reference lives under the API tab.

searchDocs.title: Install​
searchDocs.content: # wally.toml [dependencies] Facet = "kr3ative/facet@0.1.0"   Or, with no toolchain at all, paste the bootstrap one-liner into the Studio command bar (enable Game Settings → Security → Allow HTTP Requests first):  local h = game:GetService("HttpService") loadstring(h:GetAsync("https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mkl48/Facet/master/dist/install.luau"))()   Either way you end up with a Facet module under ReplicatedStorage.

searchDocs.title: Define a controller​
searchDocs.content: local Facet = require(game.ReplicatedStorage.Facet) local Enums = Facet.Enums local Wall = Facet.Define({ Type = Enums.ControllerType.Absolute, VoxelSize = 2, Host = workspace.Wall, })   Host scopes every Shatter call on this controller to workspace.Wall without naming a target each time. Skip it and pass the target explicitly per call instead:Controller:Shatter(workspace.Wall, config).

searchDocs.title: Shatter it​
searchDocs.content: Wall:Shatter({ Hitbox = { Size = Vector3.new(4, 4, 4), CFrame = impactCFrame, HitboxType = Enums.HitboxType.Partial, }, Cleanup = Enums.Cleanup.Fade, }):Next(Enums.Event.NewVoxel):Then(function(result) print(#result.Voxels.Partial, "voxels produced") end)   See DefineConfig andShatterConfig for every option.

searchDocs.title: Next​
searchDocs.content: Head to Hitboxes to see how detection actually picks parts.

searchDocs.title: How detection works​
searchDocs.content: Every candidate part under the target gets indexed into a broad-phase octree built fresh for that one call (src/VoxelController/Octree.luau). The octree narrows candidates down by bounding sphere; an exact geometric test then runs on whatever it returns, picked byQueryType:  QueryType	Exact test	NotesBox (default)	oriented box vs. part overlap	respects the hitbox's CFrame rotation Sphere	distance from CFrame.Position vs. radius	Size is read as a radius Region	axis-aligned bounding box overlap	ignores the hitbox's rotation Raycast	a single workspace:Raycast along ViewVector	one hit only - see Caveats  Because detection runs through the octree instead of workspace:GetPartBoundsInBox, it doesn't depend on the candidate's CanQuery/CanCollide or even on it living in workspace.

searchDocs.title: HitboxType​
searchDocs.content: Partial (the default) slices whatever the hitbox finds into a voxel grid, splitting the result into Partial (inside the hitbox) and Extra (outside it, greedy-meshed). Wholeskips slicing entirely for parts the hitbox fully contains - those come back untouched inResult.Voxels.Whole, each one still carrying a :Shatter() you can call later with no hitbox needed.

searchDocs.title: Caveats​
searchDocs.content: Raycast casts once through the engine and only resolves the single closest hit alongViewVector - there's no multi-hit walk through everything the ray passes through. If you need every part along a ray, run your own workspace:Raycast loop and pass the resulting parts through as a Region/Box hitbox per hit instead.

searchDocs.title: Indiscriminate destruction (no Target)​
searchDocs.content: Every example above scopes the hitbox to one target's descendants. Drop the Target argumentand leave the controller without a Host, and the hitbox becomes the only scope - Shatterqueries the engine directly (workspace:GetPartBoundsInBox/GetPartBoundsInRadius, orworkspace:Raycast) instead of narrowing a known candidate list through the octree:  local Demolition = Facet.Define({ Type = Enums.ControllerType.Relative, RecommendedVoxelSize = 1 }) Demolition:Shatter({ Hitbox = { Size = Vector3.new(24, 24, 24), CFrame = explosionCFrame, QueryType = Enums.QueryType.Sphere, Scope = workspace.City, -- optional - defaults to the whole workspace }, })   A Hitbox is required for this form - omitting both Target and Hitbox leaves Shatter with no scope at all and it errors. Scope (default the whole workspace) restricts the native query to one instance's descendants, the same way a Target would for the scoped form above.Result.Instance comes back nil, since no single Target/Host exists for this call.

searchDocs.title: Why debris sometimes doesn't move​
searchDocs.content: A produced voxel starts out with the same Anchored value the part you shattered had. If that part was Anchored = true (the normal case for a static wall or building), every voxel comes out anchored too - and an anchored part ignores AssemblyLinearVelocity entirely, soExplode/Whitehole/Scatter silently do nothing until something unanchors the part. Every Effect below that gives a voxel velocity sets Anchored = false on it for you, exceptExplode itself (it only sets velocity - pair it with Anchored = false explicitly, or useChunk/Whitehole/Scatter instead, which unanchor on their own).

searchDocs.title: Explode = { Radius: number }​
searchDocs.content: Pushes the voxel directly away from the Shatter origin (the hitbox center, or the explicit point for indiscriminate destruction) at Radius studs/sec. Does not unanchor the part - pair it with Anchored = false or Chunk = true.

searchDocs.title: Anchored = boolean​
searchDocs.content: Sets the produced part's Anchored directly, overriding whatever the original part's Anchoredwas. Chunk unanchors on its own regardless of this key, so combining the two only matters if you want Anchored = true to win and keep the assembly frozen in place.

searchDocs.title: Chunk = boolean | { BreakSpeed: number?, BreakRadius: number? }​
searchDocs.content: Effects = { [Enums.Effects.Chunk] = true } -- or, tuned: Effects = { [Enums.Effects.Chunk] = { BreakSpeed = 30, BreakRadius = 6 } }   This is the one to reach for when you want an actual structural collapse, not just debris flying everywhere. Every voxel one Shatter call produced (Partial and Extra) gets welded together into a single unanchored rigid assembly - one piece, physically - instead of every tiny voxel tumbling apart on its own. Cut the bottom floor out of a building with Chunk = true and the remaining Extra geometry above it has nothing left holding it up: it falls and lands as one piece, the way an actual building section would.  It doesn't stay one rigid lump forever, though - when the assembly's relative impact speed against whatever it lands on reaches BreakSpeed studs/sec (default 40), the weld at that contact point breaks, detaching that voxel and every other still-welded voxel withinBreakRadius studs of it (default 4) - so a hard enough landing fractures the assembly into a handful of smaller chunks, while a soft landing leaves it intact. Detached chunks keep listening for their own hard impacts, so a chunk that breaks off and then slams into something else can fracture further.  Combine it with Explode/Slice for a more dramatic initial kick before gravity takes over - just expect the welded pieces to settle into one shared velocity almost immediately, since they're rigidly connected.

searchDocs.title: Slice = { Normal: Vector3?, Speed: number? }​
searchDocs.content: Splits voxels into two groups by which side of a plane (through the Shatter origin, facingNormal, default Vector3.new(0, 1, 0)) they landed on, then pushes each group apart alongNormal at Speed studs/sec (default 8) - one half goes +Normal, the other -Normal. Unanchors automatically.  -- a horizontal cut: the top half launches up, the bottom half launches down Effects = { [Enums.Effects.Slice] = { Normal = Vector3.new(0, 1, 0), Speed = 14 } }

searchDocs.title: Blackhole = { Position: Vector3?, Duration: number? }​
searchDocs.content: Tweens the voxel into Position (default the Shatter origin) while shrinking it to nothing overDuration seconds (default 1), then destroys it (or releases it back to the Pool ifObjectPooling is on). Unanchors and disables CanCollide so nothing blocks its path inward.

searchDocs.title: Whitehole = { Position: Vector3?, Speed: number? }​
searchDocs.content: The mirror of Blackhole without the despawn: launches the voxel directly away from Position(default the Shatter origin) at Speed studs/sec (default 16). Unanchors automatically. Reads like Explode, but takes a fixed launch speed instead of scaling with a radius, and an explicit point instead of always using the Shatter origin.

searchDocs.title: Scatter = { Speed: number? }​
searchDocs.content: Launches the voxel in a random direction at Speed studs/sec (default 10) - useful for messy, non-directional debris where Explode's radial-from-origin pattern looks too uniform. Unanchors automatically.

searchDocs.title: Shrink = { Duration: number? }​
searchDocs.content: Tweens the voxel's Size down to nothing over Duration seconds (default 0.6), then destroys (or releases) it. Doesn't touch position/velocity/Anchored - combine it with another Effect above if you also want it moving while it shrinks.

searchDocs.title: A raw Velocity​
searchDocs.content: The plain string key "Velocity" (not an Enums.Effects member) setsAssemblyLinearVelocity to whatever Vector3 you give it directly, with no unanchoring and no origin math - an escape hatch for when none of the named Effects above fit:  Effects = { Velocity = Vector3.new(0, 20, 0) }
